title: P&C Clientwise — Competitors Serving P&C Insurance Clients
grain: one row per competitor engaged at a given P&C insurance client (client-to-competitor pairing)
summary: Lists, for each P&C insurance client, the competing service providers active at that client, an approximate size of each competitor's footprint (headcount or revenue), the segment/function the competitor supports, and free-text research notes on what the competitor does there. It lets you see which competitors are present at a named client and roughly how large their presence is.
use_cases: Which competitors are engaged at a given P&C client (e.g. Allstate)?
At which P&C clients does a specific competitor (e.g. Accenture) appear?
What is the approximate FTE or revenue footprint of a competitor at a client?
Which functions or segments does a competitor support at a P&C client?
How many distinct competitors are listed against each P&C client?

columns.name: Client Name
columns.note: P&C insurance client; populated only on the first row of each client's block, so 77% null — forward-fill needed to attribute the competitor rows below it to the correct client

columns.name: Competitor
columns.note: competing service provider at the client; value 'Captive' denotes an in-house/captive operation rather than a named third-party vendor

columns.name: FTEs/Revenue
columns.note: approximate size of the competitor's footprint; mixed units and ranges stored as text (e.g. '300-500 FTEs', '$2.5-3M', 'Unknown'), not a clean numeric measure

columns.name: Segment supported by competitor
columns.note: function/segment the competitor supports; mostly empty

columns.name: CoPilot Research
columns.note: multi-line research notes on the competitor's role across insurance value-chain functions; mostly empty

not_answerable.q: What is the P&C client's own premium or revenue?
not_answerable.reason: no column carries the client's financials; FTEs/Revenue describes the competitor's footprint, not the client

not_answerable.q: How has a competitor's footprint at a client changed over time?
not_answerable.reason: there is no date or time-series column; the table is a single snapshot

not_answerable.q: What is EXL's own scope or revenue at these clients?
not_answerable.reason: no column captures the host provider's engagement, only competitors' presence

warnings: vintage_unknown — no date found in filename, sheet names, headers, or cell values; data owner should confirm the as-of date
near-duplicate column set with 'L&A Clientwise' — same schema but distinct data (P&C clients vs L&A clients); not a duplicate
Client Name is sparse (77% null): the client name appears only on the first row of each client's competitor block, so forward-fill is required to associate competitor rows with their client
